steps.title: Find the graphed line
steps.content:
The graph shown is $y=f(x)-11$. Using two points on that line, $(-2,0)$ and $(0,-4)$, the slope is
$$
\frac{-4-0}{0-(-2)}=-2
$$
The $y$-intercept is $-4$, so the graphed line is $y=-2x-4$.

steps.title: Undo the shift
steps.content:
Since $y=f(x)-11$, add $11$ to the graphed line:
$$
f(x)=(-2x-4)+11=-2x+7
$$

steps.title: Match the equation
steps.content: The choice that defines $f(x)$ as $-2x+7$ is the match. The answer is B.
